sections.content: Now I can see the player (N) is inside the frame area. The frame (W) is to the left of the player, and the blue box (b) is also to the left. The frame seems to have expanded and now the player is inside it. Let me check the segmentation to understand the current layout.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
for n in nodes:
    if n['id'] not in [0, 11, 12]:
        print(f"  id={n['id']:2d} color={n['color']} boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The frame (W) is at cols 28-32, the player (N) is at cols 33-35, and the blue box (b) is at cols 29-30. The frame expanded further left. The blue box is still inside the frame.
So the mechanic seems to be: when the player moves in a direction, the frame expands in that direction. The frame is "following" the player.
Let me try pressing SPACE now while the player is inside/near the frame to see if something different happens.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try SPACE now that player is inside the expanded frame
action(['SPACE'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
